summary_strategy: Reframe security engineering experience as developer-centric systems work—emphasizing reliability, modularity, and low-overhead tooling to align with Embrace’s SDK goals
skills_strategy: Use condensed 3-line format prioritizing Security Engineering & Automation, Cloud/Platform Integrations (highlighting CI/CD, Docker, AWS), and Security Data/AI (RAG, Pandas, vector DBs) to mirror Android SDK needs: stability, integrability, and observability

experience_focus.must_highlight: developer-facing tooling, operational reliability, cross-platform automation
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: No invented metrics (e.g., 200 hours saved), no unverified tool ownership (e.g., 'built Coraline'), no speculative SDK experience

risk_controls: Never invent project names, metrics, or internal tools—only use explicit resume content
Avoid phrases like 'pioneered' or 'established the technical standard'; replace with factual verbs like 'built', 'developed', 'implemented'
Do not assume Kotlin/NDK experience; instead, emphasize transferable systems understanding (e.g., 'deep appreciation for runtime constraints and lifecycle management')
